questions.q: Describe the process of double fertilization in angiosperms.
questions.answer: Double fertilization is a unique feature of angiosperms. It involves the fusion of two male gametes with two female gametes. The first male gamete fuses with the egg cell to form a diploid zygote, which develops into an embryo. The second male gamete fuses with the polar nuclei of the secondary nucleus to form a triploid primary endosperm nucleus, which develops into endosperm.
marking_scheme: Describe the first male gamete fusion with the egg cell (2 marks)
Describe the second male gamete fusion with the polar nuclei (2 marks)
Explain the development of zygote and endosperm (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the role of male and female gametophytes in the process of sexual reproduction in angiosperms.
questions.answer: The male gametophyte produces microspores, which develop into male gametes. The female gametophyte produces megaspores, which develop into female gametes. The male gametophyte is a pollen grain, which germinates to form a pollen tube. The pollen tube carries the male gametes towards the ovary of the flower. The female gametophyte is the embryo sac, which contains the egg cell and other female gametes.
marking_scheme: Describe the male gametophyte and its role (2 marks)
Describe the female gametophyte and its role (2 marks)
Explain the function of the pollen tube (1 mark)

questions.q: Describe the structure and function of the ovary in a flower.
questions.answer: The ovary is the female reproductive organ of a flower. It is the part of the flower that contains the ovules. The ovary is usually inferior but can be superior. It is divided into locules, which are the compartments where the ovules are located. The ovary produces ovules, which develop into seeds. The ovary also produces nectar, which attracts pollinators.
marking_scheme: Describe the structure of the ovary (2 marks)
Explain the function of the ovary (2 marks)
Mention the types of ovary (1 mark)

questions.q: A flower has 8 stamens and 2 carpels. Calculate the total number of microspores and megaspores produced by the flower.
questions.answer: Calculation: Each stamen produces 4 microspores, so 8 stamens will produce 8 x 4 = 32 microspores. Each carpel produces 1 megaspore, so 2 carpels will produce 2 megaspores. Therefore, the total number of microspores produced by the flower is 32, and the total number of megaspores produced is 2.
marking_scheme: Calculate the number of microspores (2 marks)
Calculate the number of megaspores (2 marks)
Explain the significance of the ratio of microspores to megaspores (1 mark)

questions.q: A flower produces 10 ovules. If each ovule develops into a seed, calculate the number of seeds produced by the flower.
questions.answer: Calculation: The flower produces 10 ovules, each of which develops into a seed. Therefore, the total number of seeds produced by the flower is 10.
marking_scheme: Calculate the number of ovules (2 marks)
Explain the development of ovules into seeds (2 marks)
Mention the significance of the number of seeds (1 mark)

questions.q: A flower has 6 stamens, each producing 2 microspores. Calculate the total number of microspores produced by the flower.
questions.answer: Calculation: Each stamen produces 2 microspores, so 6 stamens will produce 6 x 2 = 12 microspores. Therefore, the total number of microspores produced by the flower is 12.
marking_scheme: Calculate the number of microspores produced by each stamen (2 marks)
Calculate the total number of microspores (2 marks)
Explain the significance of the number of microspores (1 mark)
